Years of Family
Coffee Farming

The Gonzalez family's land.
One unbroken story.

The Gonzalez family has farmed coffee in Boquete, Panama for over 90 years. Every bean we source comes from land my family owns today, or land my grandfather once farmed with his own hands. These are not anonymous origins — they are fields with family names, worked by people we know, on soil we have tended for nearly a century.

Read the full story →
Gonzalez Family · Single-Origin Coffee

The Gonzalez Family
Finca Gonzalez · Gonzalez Family · Boquete, Panamá

Coffee Cherry Tea

Cascara
by the pound

Gonzalez Family · Finca Gonzalez · Boquete, Panamá

Long before cascara had a name in specialty coffee, our family was brewing it. The sun-dried skin of the coffee cherry — naturally sweet, fruity, and earthy. A taste of the whole plant, not just the bean.

How to Brew

Hot: 2 tbsp per 8oz at 190°F, steep 4 minutes.
Cold: 3 tbsp per 12oz cold water, steep overnight, strain and serve over ice.

Half Pound
8

8oz  ·  Dried Coffee Cherry  ·  Boquete Origin  ·  ~15–20 servings

Hibiscus Tamarind Rose Hip Natural Sweet

2

Green Beans · Direct Sourcing

Are you a roaster?
We source direct.

Full access to unroasted green beans from Finca Gonzalez, Boquete, Panama. Three varieties. Complete origin documentation. Direct from our family's land. No middlemen. No markup.
